name: Bexte and Zesch Acceptable Cold-start Relabeling Extract
population_or_context: Supervised content scoring for short-answer student responses using the Student Response Analysis dataset.
sample_size: For each of five labels, 100 answers were generated; experiments compare generated SRA-gen training data with original student-authored SRA answers.
methods: Extracted generated target-label design, label-conformance analysis, model training on generated data, and manual relabeling of generated answers.
education_context: Short-answer content scoring with a five-label SRA label scale.
response_origin: LLM-generated short answers are created in parallel to a student-authored SRA dataset and used as candidate training data for content-scoring models.
label_source: Initial labels are prompt-requested target labels; the study manually relabels generated answers to clean noisy labels.
generation_model: DeepSeek-v2, a 4-bit quantized mixture-of-experts model with 15.7B parameters, accessed through Ollama 0.5.7.
generation_method: Zero-shot answer generation requests 100 answers per desired label for each dataset question.
prompt_strategy: The prompt includes the question and a description of the desired label; default model parameters are used.
evaluation_method: Label-conformance analysis and macro-averaged F1 on original student answers, comparing as-generated labels, manually cleaned labels, and original SRA training data.
scorer_use: Potentially acceptable for cold-start training support after label audit or manual relabeling; not acceptable as label-free scoring evidence or as a validation set.
real_response_comparator: Original SRA student-authored answers provide the test set and comparison point for models trained on generated answers.
reported_metrics: Macro-averaged F1 and label-conformance analysis; the paper reports up to 75% nonconformance to requested labels.
limitations: Generated target labels are unreliable without audit.
Manual relabeling adds human labor and weakens claims of label-free synthetic training.
The evidence is bounded to one short-answer content-scoring setup and one generation workflow.
